name: twilio
context: Twilio communications platform — SMS, voice calls, WhatsApp messaging, phone verification, and TwiML
info: The Twilio ecosystem covers programmable communications: sending and receiving SMS/MMS, making and receiving voice calls, WhatsApp Business messaging, phone and email verification via OTP, and building interactive voice response (IVR) flows with TwiML. This tree is organized around the jobs an agent actually performs: sending text messages, placing outbound calls, verifying user phone numbers, and composing TwiML documents for call routing. The Twilio Node.js helper library is the primary SDK. All API interactions use Account SID and Auth Token for authentication over HTTPS.
body:
# Twilio Ecosystem

Twilio is a cloud communications platform where agents send SMS messages, make voice calls, send WhatsApp messages, verify phone numbers, and build interactive voice response (IVR) flows. The platform exposes REST APIs for every communication channel, backed by a global telephony network.

## Agent Jobs

The primary tasks an agent performs in this ecosystem:

- **Send SMS** — deliver text or MMS messages to any phone number worldwide. See [[twilio/sms]].
- **Make voice calls** — place outbound calls with dynamic TwiML or recorded audio. See [[twilio/voice]].
- **Send WhatsApp messages** — reach users on WhatsApp via the Business API. See [[twilio/whatsapp]].
- **Verify phone numbers** — send OTP codes via SMS, voice, email, or WhatsApp for 2FA. See [[twilio/verify]].
- **Build IVR flows** — compose TwiML documents that gather input, play audio, record, and route calls. See [[twilio/twiml]].

## API Overview

All Twilio APIs use REST over HTTPS with Basic Auth (Account SID : Auth Token). Responses are JSON by default. The base URL is `https://api.twilio.com/2010-04-01/Accounts/{AccountSid}/`.

| Resource | Endpoint | Purpose |
|----------|----------|----------|
| Messages | `/Messages.json` | Send and retrieve SMS/MMS/WhatsApp |
| Calls | `/Calls.json` | Make and manage voice calls |
| Verify | `https://verify.twilio.com/v2/Services/{ServiceSid}/Verifications` | Send and check OTP codes |

## Authentication

Every request requires two credentials stored as environment variables:

- **TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID** — your account identifier (starts with `AC`)
- **TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN** — your secret auth token

For sub-accounts or API keys, use the API Key SID and Secret instead.

## Phone Numbers

Twilio phone numbers are the origination point for SMS and voice. Purchase numbers through the Console or API. Numbers are region-specific and support different capabilities (SMS, MMS, voice, fax). Use Messaging Services to pool numbers for high-volume SMS.

## Libraries

- **twilio** (Node.js) — official helper library for JS/TS
- **twilio-python** — official Python helper library
- **twilio-go** — official Go helper library
- **twilio-cli** — command-line interface for managing resources

sections.title: API overview
sections.body: Twilio exposes REST APIs over HTTPS. All requests authenticate with Account SID and Auth Token via HTTP Basic Auth. The base URL is `https://api.twilio.com/2010-04-01/Accounts/{AccountSid}/`. Messages, Calls, and other resources are sub-paths. Responses are JSON. Rate limits vary by endpoint and account tier. Use the `twilio` Node.js helper library for idiomatic access.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.body: Primary auth uses Account SID (`TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID`, starts with `AC`) and Auth Token (`TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN`) as HTTP Basic Auth credentials. For more granular access, create API Keys in the Console — each key has a SID (starts with `SK`) and a Secret. API Keys can be revoked independently without rotating the master Auth Token. Never commit credentials to source control.

sections.title: Phone numbers
sections.body: Twilio phone numbers are required for sending SMS (see [[twilio/sms]]) and making calls (see [[twilio/voice]]). Purchase numbers via the Console or the IncomingPhoneNumbers API. Each number has capabilities: SMS, MMS, voice, fax. Use Messaging Services to pool multiple numbers for throughput and compliance. For WhatsApp, use the Twilio WhatsApp sandbox or a registered WhatsApp Business number (see [[twilio/whatsapp#setup]]).
